title: Design Graph
description: Design a directed graph using adjacency list. Support: addEdge(src, dst), removeEdge(src, dst), hasPath(src, dst) using DFS or BFS.
key_concept: Adjacency list: Map<node, Set<neighbor>>. Space O(V+E). DFS/BFS for path existence. Visited set prevents infinite loops in cyclic graphs.

hints.L1: Use a Map (or object) where each key is a node, value is a Set of neighbors. hasPath uses BFS or DFS with a visited set.
hints.L2: hasPath(src, dst): BFS with queue. Pop node, if == dst return true. Add unvisited neighbors. Mark visited immediately on enqueue to avoid re-processing.
hints.L3: DFS hasPath: visited=set(); stack=[src]; while stack: n=pop; if n==dst: return true; if n in visited: continue; visited.add(n); push all neighbors
